title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
summary: Baseten's research team presents methods for managing memory in long-horizon agents by compressing the KV cache to enable near-lossless retrieval of relevant context during inference. The approach covers selection versus synthesis strategies, amortization, sparse autoencoders, iterative compaction, and characterization of compressed caches as MLPs.
writeup: Baseten's research team addresses memory management in long-horizon agents by compressing the KV cache to allow near-lossless retrieval of relevant context at inference time. The talk contrasts selection versus synthesis strategies and walks through amortization, sparse autoencoders, and iterative compaction. It also characterizes compressed caches as MLPs. The work matters because long-running agents accumulate context that strains memory, and these methods aim to preserve relevant information while reducing cache size.
